title: LoRA-Switch: Boosting the Efficiency of Dynamic LLM Adapters via System-Algorithm Co-design

problem.description: Dynamic adapters introduce significant latency overhead during inference
problem.cause: Excessive CUDA kernel calls (4+ additional calls per layer) and prolonged context operations
problem.impact: Substantial extra CUDA kernel execution latency exceeding actual computation time

solution.method: MoE-based dynamic adapters structure with token-wise adapter routing
solution.mechanism: Each token is associated with k weighted paths of LoRA adapters activated prior to decoding
solution.goal: Reduce the number of CUDA kernel calls and improve inference efficiency

context.background: Dynamic adapters are lightweight adapters that can be selectively fine-tuned to enhance LLM capabilities
context.benefit: They maintain original model strengths while substantially increasing adaptability and capacity
context.parameter_impact: Typically add only 1-5% of the original model's parameters and computing complexity
